data.title: REV Group rallies after posting a beat-and-raise quarter
data.description:
REV Group (NYSE:REVG) tracked higher in early action on Wednesday after the Wisconsin-based company beat FQ2 earnings expectations.

Revenue was up 2.0% year-over-year to $629.1 million. Excluding the impact of the Bus Manufacturing Businesses, sales increased 7.7% compared to the prior year quarter. The increase was primarily due to higher net sales in the Specialty Vehicles segment, partially offset by lower net sales in the Recreational Vehicles segment.

Excluding the impact of the Bus Manufacturing Businesses, adjusted EBITDA was up 63.6% compared to a year ago. The increase was primarily due to the higher contribution from the Specialty Vehicles segment and lower contribution from the Recreational Vehicles segment.

"The standout this quarter was the sustained year-over-year increase in manufacturing throughput within the fire group, which played a pivotal role in driving our top-line growth," stated REV Group (NYSE:REVG) CEO, Mark Skonieczny. "Within the quarter we utilized our robust cash flow and financial position to repurchase $88 million of shares, which we view as an attractive use of capital. Building on the performance demonstrated through the first half of the fiscal year, we also plan to accelerate certain capital investments to further our manufacturing throughput goals including a $20 million investment in our Brandon, South Dakota location," he added.

REV Group (REVG) raises guidance for FY25, now seeing FY25 revenue of $2.35 billion to $2.45 billion (midpoint $2.40 billion), vs. $2.38 billion consensus and up from prior guidance of $2.30 billion to $2.40 billion.

Shares of REV Group (REVG) rallied 5.5% in premarket trading on Wednesday, after gaining 1.9% on Tuesday.

data.title: Old Dominion Freight Line reports decline in May revenue per day
data.description:
Old Dominion Freight Line (NASDAQ:ODFL) announced on Wednesday a decline in its revenue per day for the month of May on the back of a continued softness in the economy and lower fuel prices.

Revenue per day decreased 5.8% year-over-year in May on an 8.4% decline in LTL tons per day. The decline was attributed to the fall of 6.8% in LTL shipments per day and 1.9% in LTL weight per shipment.

"Our revenue results for May reflect continued softness in the domestic economy as well as the impact of lower fuel prices on our yields," said the cargo transportation company's CEO Marty Freeman.

Shares were +0.19% pre-market to $163.06.

data.title: Juggernaut Exploration secures $1.1M financing to advance Big One gold-silver project
data.description:
• Juggernaut Exploration (TSXV:JUGR:CA) has fully subscribed a $1.1M hard dollar financing round.
  • The funding supports exploration at the Big One property in BC’s Golden Triangle, near Newmont’s Galore Creek.
  • The project includes a newly discovered 11 km “Highway of Gold” around the Eldorado porphyry system.
  • Assays from the Big One discovery returned up to 79.01 g/t gold and 3,157.89 g/t silver from over 200 veins.
  • The financing includes 1.72M units at $0.64 each, with a warrant exercisable at $0.84 for 60 months.
  • Proceeds will be used for general working capital.
  • Financing is expected to close on June 13, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: RFK Jr. says Moderna agreed to another clinical trial to support new COVID shot
data.description:
Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) has agreed to conduct another placebo-controlled clinical trial for its newly approved next-gen COVID-19 vaccine, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has said.

Late last week, the FDA approved the messenger RNA-based shot branded as mNEXSPIKE for all adults aged 65 and above, as well as those aged 12–64 years with at least one underlying risk factor.

The approval was based on data from an active-controlled Phase 3 clinical trial in which mNEXSPIKE generated a higher immune response against COVID-19 compared to Moderna’s (NASDAQ:MRNA) original COVID shot, Spikevax.

“I want to address those of you who have anxieties about FDA's limited approval of a new mRNA COVID vaccine for high-risk populations,” RFK Jr. said in an X post on Tuesday, adding, “Moderna has agreed to a true placebo-controlled trial of the new vaccine.”

“The FDA will monitor and collect data throughout the trial for every adverse outcome—not just a table list of expected outcomes,” he noted, adding, “The FDA will scrutinize every aspect of the trial.” Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) was not immediately available for Seeking Alpha’s requests for comments.

data.title: American Express launches exclusive lounge in Montréal for Formula 1 visitors
data.description:
American Express (NYSE:AXP) announced on Wednesday the launch of an exclusive lounge in Montréal Maison Amex by the payment company's Canadian unit, the official Formula 1 payments partner.

The cardmembers visiting Montreal for F1 will have access to Maison Amex offered at the track from June 12 to 15 (FORMULA 1 PIRELLI GRAND PRIX DU CANADA 2025 race weekend).

Shares were +0.37% pre-market to $298.50.

data.title: HP Enterprise jumps after 'solid' Q2 keeps analysts positive
data.description:
Shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) climbed about 7% premarket on Wednesday after fiscal second-quarter results beat estimates which also drew positive reactions from analysts.

Evercore kept its Outperform rating with a $22 price target on the stock.

Analysts led by Amit Daryanani said that Hewlett Packard reported "solid upside" to the April-quarter expectations, reporting revenues/EPS of $7.63B/$0.38 ahead of consensusat $7.45B/$0.33. About 5 cents EPS upside was driven by lower tariff impact and organic execution.

The analysts added that they think Hewlett Packard is poised to see sustained upside bias with or without an activist over the next few quarters. With multiple catalysts ahead – "HPE discover event where we should get multiple new product launches, [Juniper Networks] (NYSE:JNPR) trial vs. DOJ starts in early July, and Analyst day on Oct 15th," said the analysts.

Daryanani and his team noted that Hewlett Packard pointed to a more positive outlook than feared going into the print, guiding July-quarter revenue of $8.35B at midpoint ahead of street at $8.22B and EPS of $0.40 to $0.45 versus consensus of $0.41. The company expects sequential growth across all of its product segments, including mid-teens quarter-over-quarter growth in servers,driven in part by the expected delivery of a large AI server order. For fiscal 2025, Hewlett Packard tightened its previously guided ranges, the analysts added.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Equal-weight rating with a $22 price target on Hewlett Packard's stock.

Analysts led by Meta Marshall said Hewlett Packard's fiscal second quarter posted upside on the top-line from upside in AI servers/storage, but more importantly executed on cost initiatives.

The analysts added that tariff/macro had minimal impact on Enterprise demand, although US Federal was as an area of softness given additional approvals required.

However, Marshall and her team noted that they still need the Juniper acquisition to close, as the biggest catalyst for the stock.

Related stocks: Elastic (ESTC), MongoDB (MDB), Pegasystems (PEGA) and Atlassian (TEAM) were largely flat premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Aimia launches normal course issuer bid to buyback shares
data.description:
• Aimia (OTCPK:AIMFF) has received TSX approval for a new normal course issuer bid, which allows Aimia to repurchase up to 5.91M common shares, or 10% of its public float.
  • The buyback period runs from June 6, 2025, to June 5, 2026.
  • The move aims to reduce the discount between Aimia’s share price and its net asset value.
  • In the 2024–2025 normal course issuer bid, Aimia repurchased and cancelled 5.84M shares at a weighted average price of $2.61.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Asana slides as results, guidance highlight weaker growth trends: analysts
data.description:
Asana (NYSE:ASAN) was in focus on Wednesday as Wall Street firms expressed concern over the work management software company's first-quarter results and guidance, citing a continued slowdown in growth.

Shares fell 9.5% in premarket trading.

The all-important billings metric missed estimates in the first-quarter and with revenue guidance lowered for the full-year, Morgan Stanley analyst Josh Baer said Asana has a difficult road ahead. “Shares are trading at ~5x EV/CY26 Sales, similar to SaaS peers, which we continue to see as too rich given lower growth and lower margin profile, more challenged mkt position, customer downsizing trends and uncertainty,” Baer wrote in a note to clients. He kept his Underweight rating on Asana and lowered his price target to $13 from $14.

Jefferies analyst Brent Thill was a bit more measured in his analysis, but said there is simply not enough artificial intelligence momentum to offset worries elsewhere.

“While Q1 beat [the] high-end of guide and ASAN signed a record $100M 3-yr deal, the renewal was at a lower [annual contract value] after slipping past Q1,” Thill explained. “[The company] also started seeing longer sales cycles with higher scrutiny. The low end of FY26 rev guide was lowered by $7M to embed risk of further macro deterioration. While AI Studio momentum is encouraging, material [revenue] is unlikely until FY27.” 

Thill kept his Hold rating and slightly raised his price target to $17 from $15. 

Bank of America analyst Michael J. Funk lowered his price target to $21 from $23, but kept his Buy rating, as he expressed a bit more optimism on Asana.

“Upside from AI Studio in 2HFY26 appears increasingly likely, in our view, and would represent a positive catalyst. Profitability progress was also impressive and ahead of Street expectations, supporting the accelerating margin expansion bull case,” Funk wrote in a note to clients.

“We believe this is warranted given Asana’s multiple levers for long-term growth, partially offset by [a] lower margin profile,” Funk added.

data.title: Kosmos Energy, Tullow Oil sign MoU to extend Ghana production licenses to 2040
data.description:
Kosmos Energy (NYSE:KOS) +1.6% pre-market after announcing an initial agreement with partners including Tullow Oil (OTCPK:TUWLF) (OTCPK:TUWOY) and authorities in Ghana to extend the licenses covering the Jubilee and Twenboa-Enyenra-Ntomme fields to 2040.

The memorandum of understanding includes approval to drill up to 20 additional wells in the Jubilee field, representing as much as $2B in spending in Ghana over the life of the licenses; the partnership expects to realize a material increase in gross proven-plus-probable reserves as a result of the extension.

The agreement includes a commitment to increase gas output from Jubilee and TEN to 130M cf/day, a reduced price for associated gas at Jubilee, and a reimbursement mechanism covering gas sales, the companies said.

The JV will now have to submit an updated plan of development for Jubilee and seek parliamentary approval for the extension plans.

Tullow (OTCPK:TUWLF) (OTCPK:TUWOY) is operator at Jubilee and the TEN fields, owning respective stakes of ~40% and 55%, while Kosmos (NYSE:KOS) owns respective ~40% and 20% at Jubilee and TEN.

data.title: Brookfield to invest up to SEK 95B in Swedish AI infrastructure
data.description:
Brookfield (NYSE:BN) (TSX:BN:CA) announced on Wednesday an up to SEK 95B investment in a Swedish artificial intelligence, or AI, infrastructure, representing one of the largest AI investments by the alternative asset manager in Europe.

The investment extends the company's partnership with the Swedish government, its public authorities, academia, and businesses in the region.

The investment will be centered on a new large AI center in Strängnäs, and Brookfield is set to sign a land allocation agreement for c.350K sqm of additional land. The additions will enable the data center site to more than double its capacity from 300MW to 750MW.
